name: Dawn of Destiny
recruits: Roy, Marcus, Alan, Lance, Wolt & Bors — Automatically, turn 1

name: The Princess of Bern
recruits: Merlinus & Ellen — Automatically, turn 1
Dieck, Thany, Ward & Lot — Automatically, turn 2

name: Late Arrival
recruits: Chad — Automatically, turn 1
Lugh — Visit north-eastern village

name: Collapse of the Alliance
recruits: Clarine — Turn 3: leaves castle; talks to Roy automatically (have Roy near castle)
Rutger — Turn 6: talk with Clarine (keep units back; he has Killing Edge)
warns: Turn 6: Rutger and reinforcements are deadly if units are near the castle

name: The Trap
recruits: Saul/Sol & Dorothy — Automatically, turn 1
Sue — 3rd room on west side (NPC); talk with Roy (bring spare Iron Bow)
Cath — Turn 6: talk with Roy (does not join yet; steal lockpick first)
warns: Loot-dense chapter — bring thieves and chest keys

name: The Rebellion of Ostia
recruits: Zealot — Turn 2: appears from eastern village; talk with Roy
Treck — Turn 2: appears with Zealot; talk with Zealot
Noah — Turn 3: appears from arena; talk with Zealot or Treck
warns: Visit EVERY village — there are 8 of them with major items

title: 🗂️ Save before Ch. 8
body: Keep Lilina alive through Ch. 8 to unlock Ch. 8x. If she dies the gaiden is skipped — and without all five gaidens you cannot reach the true ending. Make a backup save here as insurance.

name: The Reunion
recruits: Astohl & Lilina — Automatically, turn 1 (move Lilina left on turn 1)
Ogier/Oujay — Turn 8: NW corner with Wendy/Gwendolyn and Barth
Wendy/Gwendolyn — Turn 8: NW corner with Ogier/Oujay and Barth
Barth — Turn 8: NW corner with Ogier/Oujay and Wendy/Gwendolyn
Cath — Turn 10: talk with Roy (does not join yet; steal lockpick)
warns: Very chest-heavy — bring thieves
KEEP LILINA ALIVE — unlocks Ch. 8x

name: The Blazing Sword
warns: Required for true ending — unlocked by keeping Lilina alive in Ch. 8

title: 🗂️ Save before Ch. 9 — Western Isles route split
body: Ch. 9 has two villages. Visiting the Killer Bow village (right side) = Route A (Ch. 10A/11A, Echidna + Larum). Visiting the Restore village (or neither) = Route B (Ch. 10B/11B, Bartre + Elffin/Elphin). Duplicate your save here to see both routes.

title: Western Isles — Route A vs. Route B

opts.label: Route A
opts.cond: Visit the Killer Bow village in Ch. 9
opts.note: Generally preferred — Echidna is more useful than Bartre, Larum is an excellent dancer

opts.label: Route B
opts.cond: Visit the Restore village (or neither village) in Ch. 9
opts.note: Elffin/Elphin is equally excellent as Larum; Bartre needs heavy investment

name: The Misty Isles
recruits: Fir — Turn 5: enemy on northern bridge; talk with Noah
Sin/Shin — Enemy; talk with Fir or Sue
items: Killer Bow (right village — Route A) OR Restore (left village — Route B)
warns: Which village you visit determines your route (see card above)
Bring Noah (Fir) and Sue (Sin/Shin) to the northern group

name: The Resistance Forces
recruits: Geese — Turn 5: NPC from eastern village; talk with Roy
Gonzales — Turn 6: enemy from castle; talk with Lilina

name: Caught in the Middle
recruits: Gonzales — Turn 6: enemy from NW; talk with Lilina
Klein — Turn 8: enemy SE; talk with Clarine
Thea/Tate — Turn 10: enemy; talk with Klein (not Thany — allies will fight and die)
items: Speedwings or Elixir (NW village — Ward/Lot get Speedwings, others get Elixir)
warns: Use Ward or Lot to visit NW and N villages for better rewards

name: Hero of the Western Isles
recruits: Larum — Automatically, turn 1
Klein — Turn 5: enemy SE; talk with Clarine
Thea/Tate — Turn 7: enemy; talk with Klein (not Thany first)
Echidna — Turn 8: NPC from eastern house; talk with Larum
items: Orion's Bolt (end, if Klein recruited + soldiers survived)
Elysian Whip (end, if Thea/Tate recruited + soldiers survived)
Hero's Crest (end, if all villages visited)
warns: Visit all villages!
Keep Klein's and Thea/Tate's allied soldiers alive for bonus end rewards

name: Escape to Freedom
recruits: Elffin/Elphin — Automatically, turn 1
Geese — Turn 5: NPC from village; talk with Roy
Bartre — Turn 9: appears west; talk with Fir (or Roy)
items: Energy Ring (end of chapter, if all civilians survived)
warns: Keep Elffin/Elphin alive — required for Ch. 12x on Route B
Protect all civilians for the Energy Ring

title: 🗂️ Save before Ch. 12 — turn limit for Ch. 12x
body: Complete Ch. 12 in ≤20 turns to unlock Ch. 12x. Route B players must also keep Elffin/Elphin alive. Save here and plan an aggressive assault.

name: The True Enemy
recruits: Raigh/Ray — Talk with Lugh or Chad (western shaman; does not move)
Cath — Turn 7: third talk with Roy joins her permanently
warns: Must complete in ≤20 turns for Ch. 12x!
Route B: also keep Elffin/Elphin alive

name: The Axe of Thunder
warns: Required for true ending

name: The Rescue Plan
recruits: Melady/Miledy & Sin/Shin — Automatically, turn 3 (Sin joins with Melady/Miledy's group)
Perceval — Enemy; talk with Larum or Elffin/Elphin (leaves end of turn 8 if not recruited)
items: Knight's Crest (end of chapter, if Perceval recruited + his soldiers survived)
warns: Get the villages for Body Ring and Al's Sword
Perceval also recruitable in Ch. 15 for hard mode stat bonuses

title: 🗂️ Save before Ch. 14 — two conditions for Ch. 14x
body: Unlock Ch. 14x by: (1) finishing in ≤25 turns AND (2) keeping Sophia alive. The Silver Card is also hidden in the desert sand here (halves all shop prices) — along with Talisman, Speedwings, Boots, Silence, Silver Blade, and Warp. Send mounted/flying units to search every tile. Forblaze is obtained at the END of Ch. 14x, not in Ch. 14 itself.

name: Arcadia
recruits: Cecilia, Sophia & Igrene — Automatically, start of chapter
Garret — Turn 3: enemy; talk with Lilina or Echidna
items: Silver Card (hidden in sand — halves shop prices forever!)
Guiding Ring (sand, requires Sophia in party)
warns: Complete in ≤25 turns AND keep Sophia alive for Ch. 14x
Search ALL desert tiles — 7 hidden items including Silver Card and Boots
Garret: talk with Lilina or Echidna before he destroys a village

name: The Infernal Element
recruits: Fa/Fae — Recruit here (required for true ending)
items: Forblaze — legendary dark tome (end of chapter)
warns: Required for true ending
Fa/Fae must be in party for the true ending

name: The Dragon Girl
recruits: Perceval — Enemy; talk with Larum or Elffin/Elphin (if not recruited in Ch. 13)
items: Knight's Crest (end, if Perceval recruited + all 4 cavalier allies survived)
warns: Hammerne is extremely rare — one of only two in the game
Recruit Perceval here for hard mode stat bonuses if you skipped Ch. 13

title: 🗂️ Save before Ch. 16 — do NOT kill Douglas
body: Douglas is an enemy general. Killing him locks you out of Ch. 16x, required for the true ending. Have Perceval talk to him to recruit him. There are also many valuable chests — bring thieves. Save here in case of accidents.

name: Retaking the Capital
recruits: Fa/Fae — Available in battle preparations
Hugh — Enemy; talk with Roy and pay 5,000–10,000G (lower pay = lower stats)
Zeiss — Turn 2: enemy; talk with Melady/Miledy
Cath — Turn 10: talk with Roy (3rd talk joins; steal lockpick first)
warns: DO NOT KILL DOUGLAS — Perceval recruits him in Ch. 16x
Delphi Shield (steal) protects against bows — very useful vs. wyverns later

name: The Pinnacle of Light
recruits: Douglas — Automatically, start of chapter (recruited via Perceval in Ch. 16)
items: Aureola — legendary light tome/staff (end of chapter)
warns: Required for true ending

title: 🗂️ Save before Ch. 17 — Sacae vs. Illia route split
body: Route is set by which pair has more total EXP: Nomads (Sue + Sin/Shin) → Sacae (Ch. 17B–20B, recruits Dayan). Pegasus Knights (Thany + Thea/Tate) → Illia (Ch. 17A–20A, recruits Juno). Check EXP in the status screen and engineer deliberately. Duplicate save to see both routes.

title: Late game — Sacae route vs. Illia route

opts.label: Sacae
opts.cond: Sue + Sin/Shin have more total EXP than Thany + Thea/Tate
opts.note: Ch. 17B → 18B → 19B → 20B → 20Bx (Bow of the Winds). Murgleis at end of 20Bx.

opts.label: Illia
opts.cond: Thany + Thea/Tate have more total EXP than Sue + Sin/Shin
opts.note: Ch. 17A → 18A → 19A → 20A → 20Ax (Spear of Ice). Maltet at end of 20Ax. Generally easier.

name: Bitter Cold
recruits: Niime/Nim — Automatically, start of chapter

name: Battle in Bulgar
recruits: Niime/Nim — Automatically, start of chapter

name: The Liberation of Illia
recruits: Juno — NPC; talk with Thany, Thea/Tate, or Zealot
items: Angelic Robe (end, if all civilians survived)

name: The Silver Wolf
recruits: Dayan — Turn 3: NPC; talk with Roy, Sue, or Sin/Shin

name: The Spear of Ice
warns: Illia route — required for true ending
Conditions: complete Ch. 20A in ≤25 turns, Thany + Thea/Tate + Juno must be alive

name: The Bow of the Winds
warns: Sacae route — required for true ending
Conditions: complete Ch. 20B in ≤25 turns, Sue + Sin/Shin + Dayan must be alive

name: The Binding Blade
recruits: Yodel — Automatically, start of chapter
items: Binding Blade — legendary sword, Roy promotes (end of chapter)
warns: Roy must seize to receive the Binding Blade and promote
WITHOUT all gaidens + Melady/Miledy + Zeiss alive → game ends here (bad ending)
Ch. 21x condition: complete in ≤30 turns, Melady/Miledy AND Zeiss must be alive

name: The Dragon's Gate
items: Apocalypse — legendary dark tome (end of chapter)
warns: True ending gate! Requires: all 5 gaidens + Ch. 21 in ≤30 turns + Melady/Miledy AND Zeiss alive

name: The Neverending Dream
recruits: Cath — Turn 15: talk with Roy (if not yet joined)

name: The Palace of Bern
recruits: Karel — Visit village with Fir or Bartre

name: Light (True Ending)
warns: Roy must be promoted (received Binding Blade in Ch. 21)
